findings.exploit_scenario: Attacker-controlled query param host is concatenated into exec.Command(sh,-c,...) -> OS command injection / RCE.
findings.recommendation: Use argv array exec.Command(ping,-c,1,host) and validate host (net.ParseIP).

findings.exploit_scenario: Query param id interpolated into SQL via f-string -> SQL injection.
findings.recommendation: Use a bound parameter: execute(... WHERE id = ?, (uid,)).

findings.exploit_scenario: Query param name interpolated into child_process.exec template -> OS command injection / RCE.

preconditions: exec.Command uses argv array, not a shell
findings.exploit_scenario: Recall candidate (unproven): host reaches exec.Command but argv-array + net.ParseIP appear to neutralize it.
findings.recommendation: Confirm net.ParseIP gate + argv-array; refute in triage.

preconditions: query uses bound parameter placeholder, not interpolation
findings.exploit_scenario: Recall candidate (unproven): id reaches execute but a bound parameter appears to prevent injection.
findings.recommendation: Confirm bound-parameter form; refute in triage.

preconditions: execFile used with argv array, no shell
findings.exploit_scenario: Recall candidate (unproven): name reaches execFile but argv-array (no shell) appears to prevent injection.
findings.recommendation: Confirm execFile argv-array, no shell:true; refute in triage.
